key_insight: High-impact hepatocellular carcinoma research in 2024 shifted from broad immunotherapy toward more specific combination regimens and growing emphasis on radiomics, imaging, and health-systems topics.
clinical_relevance: This bibliometric shift may help researchers and clinicians anticipate where HCC evidence is concentrating, but it does not by itself show which therapies improve patient outcomes.
method: Top 50 most cited HCC papers from 2023 and 2024 were selected using OpenAlex and 18-month post-publication citation counts.
Large language models extracted and normalized concepts from paper text into canonical topics and parent themes.
Year-over-year topic frequencies were compared to identify thematic change across the two cohorts.
evidence_signal: The analysis covered 100 influential papers and found a measurable year-over-year change in topic frequency, with general immunotherapy declining relative to CTLA-4-based combinations and diagnostic/health-systems themes rising.
watch_item: Citation-based sampling and AI text classification can miss newer or less-cited but important work, so the results should be interpreted as a trend signal rather than a complete map of the field.
paper_title: Mapping Topic Change in Influential Hepatocellular Carcinoma Research: A Two-Cohort Bibliometric Analysis
